title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, is accused of shooting his girlfriend's mother, 42, on Mother's Day in Dorchester.
The incident left the woman with life-threatening injuries; another man, also shot, sustained non-life-threatening wounds.
Broomes was arrested, arraigned without bail, and faces charges including assault with intent to murder.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Five former Red Sox pitchers, including Chris Sale and Nathan Eovaldi, have a combined 2.74 ERA in 2026.
The 2026 theoretical lineup of ex-Red Sox includes Mookie Betts, Xander Bogaerts, and MLB home run leader Kyle Schwarber.
Ex-Red Sox players, such as Dominic Smith (.353 average), outperform Boston's current roster, raising team competitiveness concerns.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roman Anthony, 22, of the Boston Red Sox is recovering from a right wrist sprain sustained on May 4.
Anthony has removed his wrist brace and played catch before the game against the Atlanta Braves on Friday.
Anthony has not resumed batting but hopes to start soon based on progress from recent throwing activities.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Jeep accidentally accelerated into BrightPath daycare in Norwood on Friday; no injuries were reported.
The daycare, located at 1210 Providence Highway, was temporarily closed for structural damage assessment.
The crash occurred when a driver failed to put the parked Jeep in gear, leading to the accidental acceleration.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict the New England Patriots will finish the 2026 season with a 10-7 record, down from a 14-3 record in 2025.
The 2026 schedule is considered challenging, featuring games against Seattle, Denver, and Chicago, contributing to a predicted record decline.
Some experts highlight key games that could impact playoff chances, while schedule difficulties are seen limiting potential winning streaks.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Marty, Life is Short' featuring Martin Short is available on Netflix, exploring personal and professional aspects of Short's life.
'Pop Culture Jeopardy!' Season 2 has moved to Netflix with Colin Jost hosting a duos format game.
'Tucci in Italy' Season 2 focuses on Italian cuisine and culture, now streaming on Disney+ and Hulu.

title: Mayor Wu remains firm as councilors consider rejecting her budget
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu presented a $4.9 billion budget for Boston, noting a challenging financial situation.
Several Boston City Council members are considering rejecting Wu's budget to push for increased spending.
Wu warned that increased spending would be fiscally irresponsible, given developments in the statewide budget.

title: Scholars bar is closing after 15 years. A new concept is already planned.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Scholars American Bistro & Cocktail Lounge in Downtown Crossing will close after 15 years, with final events this weekend.
The space at 25 School St. will reopen as Off The Rails Boston, focusing on live music and elevated Americana-inspired food.
Boston's downtown scene adjusts post-pandemic as hybrid work schedules impact weekday business traffic.

title: How much are you spending to attend the World Cup?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston will host seven FIFA World Cup 2026 matches at Boston Stadium starting June 13.
Commuter Rail round-trip tickets from South Station to Foxborough for match days are priced at $80 per person.
Match-day parking near the stadium costs approximately $175, with hotels and Airbnbs seeing price increases.

title: A first look at the massive outdoor bar coming to Cape Cod this summer
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Backyard by Foley’s, a 33,650 square-foot outdoor venue, is set to open in Dennis Port, Cape Cod, this summer.
Features include a 5,000 square-foot sail-covered main bar, picnic tables, food trucks, and activity zones for kids.
The venue will offer live music, outdoor movies, and collaborations with local businesses for pop-up events and markets.
